text.title: Structured Output — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns when integrating structured output.

failure-mode.description: Regex-extracting JSON from natural prose — model wraps JSON in markdown fence; regex misses it; production parse-fail rate 5-15%.
failure-mode.detector: code base contains `re.search(r"```json"...)` OR `output.split("{")[1]` patterns AND provider supports response_format
failure-mode.repair: Switch to provider-native structured output. OpenAI: `response_format={"type": "json_schema", ...}`. Anthropic: tool use with JSON schema.

failure-mode.description: Infinite retry loop on parse failure — burns budget, never resolves; downstream call times out.
failure-mode.detector: retry count unbounded OR > 1 in safe-parse wrapper
failure-mode.repair: Cap at exactly 1 repair retry per r3. Beyond that, log raw + return error to caller.

failure-mode.description: Schema drift — Pydantic model changed but production prompt still references old field. Model returns old shape; parser silently fills with defaults.
failure-mode.detector: git history shows Pydantic field changed within last 30 days; production logs show old field-set in responses
failure-mode.repair: Add a strict-validation flag; reject extra/missing fields explicitly. Version schemas and pass version_id to model.

failure-mode.description: Recursive schema — `class Node: children: list[Node]` — provider API rejects at call time; production breaks.
failure-mode.detector: schema JSON contains `$ref` pointing back to ancestor
failure-mode.repair: Flatten: store `nodes: list[Node]` + `parent_id: Optional[str]`. Reconstruct tree client-side.

failure-mode.description: Silent fallback — parse fails, function returns None or default; downstream code runs on empty data without warning.
failure-mode.detector: safe-parse catches all exceptions and returns default; no log/alert
failure-mode.repair: Log raw response + schema on second failure (r4). Surface to error sink + dashboard. Never return silent default.
